tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: Review the provided chest CT image and find which lobe has mass present

dialogs.thought: Check for laterality markers or labels to orient left vs right before describing findings.
Get a global description of modality/anatomy and any obvious focal lesion.

content.content: Axial chest CT image in lung windows. There is a large heterogeneous mass-like opacity in the left lung (right side of image, marked by an 'L'), with internal lucent/cavitary areas and adjacent air-filled bronchi; the remainder of the lungs show branching vascular/bronchial markings.

arguments.attribute: Describe the abnormality in this left lung region, including presence of cavitation/cysts and air bronchograms.

dialogs.thought: Confirm the suspected left-sided mass region and characterize internal features (cavitation/cystic change, air bronchograms).

content.content: Within the boxed left lung region there is a large mass-like consolidation with multiple internal round/irregular low-attenuation lucencies consistent with cavitary/cystic components. Several tubular/branching air-density structures coursing through the opacity are compatible with air bronchograms.

dialogs.content: The chest CT scan shows a large mass in the posterior segment of the left upper lobe and the superior segment of the left lobe of the lung. Additionally, the scan reveals multiple cavitary or cystic lesions and air bronchograms.
